label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004. So 2001 isn't. So Jane was born on 02/28/2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. So that would be 16 years later. Adding 16 years to 2001 gives 2017. So today's date is February 28, 2017. Wait, but is 2017 a leap year? Let me check. 2016 was a leap year, then next is 2020. So 2017 isn't. So today is 02/28/2017. The question asks for the date 10 days ago. So if today is February 28, 2017, subtracting 10 days would take us back to... Let's count. February has 28 days in 2017. So if we go back 10 days from the 28th, we subtract 10. 28 minus 10 is 18. So that would be February 18, 2017. So the date 10 days ago would be 02/18/2017, which is option B. Wait, but let me double-check. Let's count backwards. Starting from today, Feb 28, 2017. 1 day before is 27th, then 26th, 25th, 24th, 23rd, 22nd, 21st, 20th, 19th, and 18th on the 10th day. Yes, that's 10 days. So 10 days ago was the 18th. So option B is 02/18/2017. That seems right. But wait, sometimes when people count days, they might include today or not. Let me make sure. If today is the 28th, then 1 day ago was the 27th. So 10 days ago would be the 28 - 10 = 18th. Yes, because each day you go back subtracts one. So yes, 10 days ago is the 18th. So option B is correct. The other options: option
